context: A Spanish supermarket is mostly self-service, but the moment you can't find something you need one sentence: "Where is...?". Pair that with the names of the things you actually buy and you can handle a whole shop on your own.
explanation: To ask where something is, use "donde esta" for one thing and "donde estan" for several: "Donde estan los tomates?" (Where are the tomatoes?). To say you want something, use "quiero" (I want) or the softer "quería" - then the item: "Quiero pan". At the till you'll hear "algo mas?" (anything else?); answer "no, gracias" or "eso es todo" (that's all).

examples.es: Algo mas? - No, eso es todo.

examples.note: Ask for the total at the till.
